source: Federal Reserve, Bureau of Labor Statistics, Bureau of Economic Analysis, FRED
dataAgeNote: RICHY MUST USE WEB SEARCH for current values of CPI, unemployment, fed funds rate, mortgage rates, gas prices. These static values are FALLBACK ONLY.

healthy.interpretation: Economy running normally. Prices rising at a manageable pace.

elevated.interpretation: Prices rising faster than normal. Budget needs adjustment. Lock in fixed costs where possible.

high.interpretation: Significant price pressure. Consider stocking up on non-perishables. Renegotiate any variable-rate contracts. Prioritize HYSA for savings.

crisis.interpretation: Severe inflation. Delay major purchases if possible. Focus on essential spending. Investment in I-bonds and TIPS.

tight_market.interpretation: Strong job market. Good time to negotiate raises or switch jobs. Employers are competing for workers.

softening.interpretation: Job market weakening. Build up emergency fund. Consider holding off on risky career moves.

recession.interpretation: Significant job losses. Prioritize emergency fund. Cut discretionary spending. Avoid major financial commitments.

low.interpretation: Low rates. Great for borrowers (cheap mortgages, refinancing). Bad for savers. Consider locking in low fixed rates.

high.interpretation: High rates. Good for savers (HYSA paying 4%+). Bad for borrowers. Avoid new variable-rate debt. Consider CDs to lock in high yields.

very_high.interpretation: Very high rates. Maximize savings yields. Minimize borrowing. Pay down variable-rate debt aggressively.

strong.interpretation: Consumers feeling good. Demand is high — prices may be elevated. Be price-conscious.

weak.interpretation: Consumers pulling back. Retailers may discount to drive sales. Good time to negotiate and find deals.

crisis.interpretation: Consumer sentiment is very low. Expect significant retail discounting. Great buying opportunities for patient shoppers.

cheap.interpretation: Gas is cheap. Multi-store shopping trips are more cost-effective.

expensive.interpretation: Gas is elevated. Consolidate errands. Factor gas cost into multi-store shopping decisions.

very_expensive.interpretation: Gas is very high. Minimize driving. One-store shopping may be cheaper than multi-store.

excellent.interpretation: Historically excellent rates. Strong time to buy if ready. Refinance if current rate is above this.

good.interpretation: Reasonable rates. Buying makes sense if staying 5+ years.

elevated.interpretation: Rates are elevated. Run numbers carefully before buying.

high.interpretation: High rates. Monthly payments significantly higher. Consider waiting if possible.

strong_expansion.interpretation: Manufacturing growing robustly. Economy is healthy. Demand is strong — don't expect many discounts on manufactured goods.

contraction.interpretation: Manufacturing shrinking but overall GDP may still be positive. Watch for discounts on durable goods as inventory builds.

severe_contraction.interpretation: Manufacturing deeply contracting. Overall GDP likely declining. Recession probable. Full defensive financial posture.

very_healthy.interpretation: Very few layoffs. Labor market is tight. Workers have leverage for raises and job switches.

healthy.interpretation: Normal level of layoffs. Stable job market.

concerning.interpretation: Layoffs accelerating. Job market softening. Build emergency fund to 6 months.

recessionary.interpretation: Significant layoffs occurring. Recession likely underway. Maximize financial defense.

tight.interpretation: Investors very confident. Credit flowing freely. No stress in financial system.

stressed.interpretation: Financial stress building. Credit tightening. Businesses finding it harder to borrow.

crisis.interpretation: Severe financial stress. Credit markets seizing. Historically a strong long-term buying signal for patient investors, but short-term pain is likely.

normal.interpretation: Labor market stable. No recession signal.

watch.interpretation: Unemployment trending up. Not yet at recession levels but worth monitoring. Strengthen emergency fund.

triggered.interpretation: Sahm Rule triggered. Historically indicates recession in progress. However, context matters — check if driven by layoffs (bad) or labor supply increase (less concerning). Regardless, strengthen financial position.

normal.interpretation: Normal upward-sloping yield curve. No recession signal from this indicator.

flat.interpretation: Yield curve is flat. Economy may be transitioning. Not alarming by itself.

inverted.interpretation: Yield curve inverted. Historically preceded every recession since 1955, but had a recent false signal (2022-2024 inversion with no recession). Take seriously but don't panic — combine with other indicators.

personalImpactFormula.description: To calculate a user's personal inflation rate: for each spending category, multiply their monthly spend by the category inflation rate. Sum all impacts and divide by total monthly spending.
personalImpactFormula.monthlyDollarImpact: For each category: (categorySpend × categoryRate / 100) = monthly extra cost vs last year
personalImpactFormula.example: User spends $600/mo on groceries at 3.2% inflation → $600 × 0.032 = $19.20/mo extra cost = $230/yr
